behavior_summary: Implements a peasant/beggar NPC with three primary gameplay interactions: being looked at (examine), being used (beg/receive donations), and being cast upon/attacked (call for help and possibly spawn guards).
PESANT3::look produces a short spoken line (bark) that differs if the NPC is dead vs alive.

PESANT3::use drives a donation flow: it inspects player inventory quantities, prompts the player for an amount (slider), attempts coin transfer via shared routines, and selects follow-up dialog and minor spawned actions based on the transfer outcome.

PESANT3::cast reacts to hostile targeting or spells by checking target state, clearing combat state, using randomness to pick vocal responses, and spawning helper routines including guard alerts when appropriate.
Proximity handlers (enterFastArea / leaveFastArea) manage short-lived proximity behavior: enter may spawn a helper routine to start nearby behavior; leave clears several stored references/state keys.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: handlers are triggered by events - U8P_PESANT3::look (Event 0), use (Event 1), cast (Event 11), enterFastArea, leaveFastArea.
PESANT3::look: check Npc::isDead() -> select 'dead beggar' vs 'beggar' string -> call Item::bark() -> suspend/return.

PESANT3::use: set per-NPC info, call FREE::2A56 to update refs, choose a greeting via urandom, spawn UI/animation/helper methods (METHOD::0E4F, FREE::1C19, METHOD::15E7), enumerate items with Item::getQuantity to compute available coins, present Item::getSliderInput to player, call FREE::20B8 to effect the transfer, call FREE::1A85 to obtain an outcome code, then branch to different gratitude/response barks and spawn finalizing methods (METHOD::1090) or other free routines.

PESANT3::cast: check Npc::getTarget(); if target==player spawn CALGUARD::0080 (guard alert) and FREE::1C19, clear in-combat flag via Npc::clrInCombat(), and choose one of multiple bark lines via urandom.
PESANT3::enterFastArea: if an info/ref key is below a threshold and NPC is alive, spawn internal helper U8P_PESANT3::0B53 to start proximity behavior.
PESANT3::leaveFastArea: clear state by calling resetRef(...) for multiple reference ids (tidy/cleanup).
open_questions: Exact semantics and id meanings of the per-NPC 'info' / reference keys used (which keys map to which gameplay timers/flags) remain inferred rather than explicitly named.
Precise behavior of several spawned shared routines (FREE::1C19, FREE::1A85, FREE::20B8, FREE::1AD0, FREE::2A56) is inferred from call context (UI, transfer, state update) but not definitively known.
Contents and behavior of the spawned helper U8P_PESANT3::0B53 are truncated in the excerpt — its full greeting/timing behavior is therefore inferred.

Whether the donation transfer can fail for reasons other than quantity (e.g., permissions, inventory locks) is not explicit; outcome branching uses FREE::1A85 return but mapping of codes to messages is inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_PESANT3::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the player's 'U8P_PESANT3::look' interaction by emitting a short bark describing the peasant as either alive ('beggar') or dead ('dead beggar'), then yield and return.
entry_conditions: Triggered by an examine/U8P_PESANT3::look event directed at the NPC (Event 0).
Uses implicit calling process id (pid) to direct Item::bark output to the viewer.
Checks Npc::isDead() to choose between two canned strings.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_PESANT3::look' (examine) event; choose and emit short bark depending on death state.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::use
function_map.role: Primary interaction/dialog: begging/donation flow, inventory enumeration, slider UI, transfer call, and multi-branch reply/cleanup.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::cast
function_map.role: React to spells/attacks: check target/combat, call for help, spawn guards, clear in-combat state, and emit randomized cries.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Proximity entry trigger: conditionally spawn short helper to start nearby behavior and set info keys.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Proximity exit trigger: clear several stored references / reset per-NPC state keys.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::0B53
function_map.role: Spawned short helper routine invoked on proximity; likely runs greeting/timing behavior (truncated in source).

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Engine/NPC query to determine death state used by multiple handlers.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit text/dialog to a target process; primary output mechanism for barks/lines.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a short-lived helper to initiate proximity-based behavior when conditions (info key threshold and alive) are met.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the NPC is dead to select appropriate bark text.
Emit the chosen 'beggar' or 'dead beggar' string to the inspecting player/process.
Skip spawning helper behavior if the NPC is dead.
Create the helper process U8P_PESANT3::0B53 to run proximity behavior.
Clear multiple per-NPC reference/info keys to tidy state when player leaves proximity.
Determine current combat/target state to decide whether to call for help or spawn guards.
Spawn a guard/ally helper when the NPC is targeted by the player (alert guards).
Spawn a short shared routine during cast reaction (timing/dialog helper).
Clear the NPC's in-combat state as part of the cast reaction.
Randomly select between alternate vocal reactions to being attacked/cast upon.
Set or update per-NPC info/state at the start of the use/donation flow.
Choose among greeting/dialog variants to add variety to the begging interaction.
Spawn an animation/UI-related method early in the use flow (likely animate or prepare UI).
Spawn a timing/dialog helper shared routine during use.
Get NPC direction to orient and spawn METHOD::15E7 for turning/animation.
Animate/turn the NPC toward the player using the retrieved direction.
Iterate inventory to sum available coin/item quantities before prompting the player.
Present a slider UI so the player chooses how many coins to give.
Perform the actual coin transfer / giving action via a shared routine.
Obtain a return/outcome code after transfer to select appropriate follow-up dialog and actions.
Spawned during dialog/confirmation; its resulting process value is inspected to continue the flow (UI/confirmation helper).
Spawn finalizing method(s) after dialog/transfer to complete interaction (UI/cleanup).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements a peasant/beggar NPC with three primary gameplay interactions: being looked at (examine), being used (beg/receive donations), and being cast upon/attacked (call for help and possibly spawn guards).
